title: Layered Undo with Restore, Reset, Revert, and References · Collaboration Transfer
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer layered undo with restore, reset, revert, and references into a new layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation.
Use restore source, staged, worktree, pathspec, patch, and conflict-stage controls while preserving unrelated changes.
Predict soft, mixed, hard, merge, keep, path-limited, and intent-to-add reset effects on HEAD, index, and worktree.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G79 is a lab in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation.

content.text: Case G79 is a lab in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Gita Undo Layer Classification condition decided your prediction.

content.value: Git Configuration, Hooks, and Credentials Manuals — Controls configuration scope and origin, protected config, safe.directory, hooks, credentials, aliases, and trust.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Undo is one Git operation whose only variable is the target commit.

options.text: For commit to the mission model, apply Gita Undo Layer Classification without its stated preconditions, omit the layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Undo Layer Classification for layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix.
State the Gita Undo Layer Classification preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-undo-recovery-verification-1w13md" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Undo Recovery Verification into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-undo-recovery-verification-1w13md
# scenario-key: trace-1w13md probe-dm31w1 boundary-13md1w owner-1w13mdx fault-1w13mdz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1w candidate=b00b1md
# hypothesis: only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available
git reset --mixed HEAD^
# expected: HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction
# observation: the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable
# changed-case: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history
# after-ref: main=a11ce1w candidate=c0ffeed
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity
# transfer-limit: preview untracked and ignored data separately before any real hard reset or clean operation
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Gita Undo Recovery Verification structural check is only one gate. layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G87 is a lab in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity. Competency probe: Preserve refs or patches, preview affected paths and commits, perform the smallest repair, and verify topology, content, behavior, and remote impact.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A successful exit status proves the undo restored the intended system behavior.

stimulus.title: Gita Undo Recovery Verification failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A successful exit status proves the undo restored the intended system behavior.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve refs or patches, preview affected paths and commits, perform the smallest repair, and verify topology, content, behavior, and remote impact.

options.text: Keep the plausible Gita Undo Recovery Verification output and hide the violated layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Gita Undo Recovery Verification without its stated preconditions, omit the layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Undo Recovery Verification for layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Gita Undo Recovery Verification condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the lab evidence note for Gita Undo Recovery Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G88 is a lab in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity. Competency probe: Preserve refs or patches, preview affected paths and commits, perform the smallest repair, and verify topology, content, behavior, and remote impact.
steps.why: Retrieving Gita Undo Recovery Verification through the concrete layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve refs or patches, preview affected paths and commits, perform the smallest repair, and verify topology, content, behavior, and remote impact.
